title: Parameter Underflow causes: More than a 8k bit replication is probably wrong: 4294967295
body:
Hello,

Consider the following SystemVerilog code:

```
module test1 (output logic [15:0] data);

parameter val = 1'b1;
parameter bits = 0;

initial
begin
  if (bits == 0) data = 'd0;
  else           data = {{(17-bits){1'b0}},{(bits-1){val}}};
end

final
begin
  $display("Data = %b", data);
end

endmodule
```
When the parameter bits == 0, data should be assigned the value 0.
Verilator attempts to handle the underflow condition on the next line (-1) but this will not occur as the else branch will not be taken.

The code complies and runs in a commercial Verilog simulator.

In verilator:

```
verilator -cc test1.sv --debug --gdbbt

warning: Cannot parse .gnu_debugdata section; LZMA support was disabled at compile time
warning: no loadable sections found in added symbol-file system-supplied DSO at 0x7ffff7ffa000
No stack.
```
